title: Bohr radius
body:
The Bohr radius (⁠
  
    
      
        
          a
          
            0
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle a_{0}}
  
⁠) is a physical constant, approximately equal to the most probable distance between the nucleus and the electron in a hydrogen atom in its ground state. It is named after Niels Bohr, due to its role in the Bohr model of an atom. Its value is 5.29177210544(82)×10−11 m. The name "bohr" was also suggested for this unit.
categories: Articles needing additional references from May 2025
